basics.name: Sam Rankin
basics.label: I am a full-stack web developer that focuses on making sites beautiful and accessible, no matter the screen size.
basics.summary:
Technology has always been a passion of mine. I love the constant change because I love learning new things and pushing the limits of what's possible. And with how much technology has become integral to our society, I feel like I'm part of something bigger when I create a website or a tool that helps a client, a coworker, or even myself in the future. As a web developer, I see it as my responsibility to make my work as beautiful, intuitive, and accessible as I possible can, no matter the screen size.

My career has spanned many sectors, from an in-house marketing team for a non-profit, to a university, to start-ups, and to full-service agencies, which is where I currently find myself. 

Starting out as an intern in an in-house marketing department gave me the room to grow as a budding developer. Coding hundreds of html emails gave me the starting point I needed to learn (real) web development, and working with writers and designers taught me the foundations of marketing and design. They showed me how brand cohesion through messaging and design elements really pull a website together to create a crucial tool for a company. 

Start-ups threw me in the deep end, teaching me what’s probably been my most valuable lesson which was how to Google anything even remotely similar to what I was trying to accomplish and implement my own version. This method allowed for a lot of creativity, out of the box thinking, and self-reliance, but was severely-lacking in good code standards that more traditional methods might cover. 

Working at a full-service agency has allowed me to work with other, more experienced, developers which helped fill in many of the gaps the being primarily self-taught created. it has also given me the chance to work on a vast array of projects and helped build a portfolio I am proud of, including:

- The Los Angelas Zoo: https://www.lazoo.org/
- RIESTER: https://www.riester.com/
- Spectrum Medical: https://spectrummedicalcareaz.com/
- Ski Pro: https://skipro.com/

I have been primarily WordPress focused most of my career, but I don’t want to be pigeon-hold into any one framework, CMS, or tool. I have also built Shopify sites and have worked on .Net sites using Umbraco and Kentico CMS systems. I don’t think I’ve come across a language I truly dislike, and I’m always up for learning more.

work.name: RIESTER
work.summary:
• Primarily works with PHP, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and Wordpress to develop full-featured websites and applications
• Converts entire designs and wireframes into functional HTML pages and sites
• Analyzes and fixes performance problems on the application.
• Helps others in the organization to develop new pages and content.
• Helps drive the acceptance of new technologies and processes, interacting with both product and platform teams.
• Assists with continuous optimizations on site performance
• Implements SEO changes and UX best practices as well as updates for keeping sites secure
• Integrates back-end services and APIs
• Architectscontentmanagementsystems(CMS)usingobjectorientedorganizational structures and modular planning to create intuitive content management.
• Optimizesapplicationformaximumspeedandscalability.
• Implements responsible web design principles to ensure that websites are responsive and
render properly across multiple devices.
• Maintains and improves websites.
• Supports users by developing documentation and assistance tools.
• Liaises with designer and UI/UX teams to ensure the viability of site elements.
• Monitors website performance, watching for traffic drops related to site usability problems and rectifying issues.
• Works with clients when needed to help strategize and solve issues.

work.name: MAAT Legal
work.position: Web Developer / IT Manager / Graphic Designer
work.summary:

work.name: Skyhook Interactive
work.summary:

work.name: Joybyte
work.summary:
• Solely developed all of the company’s complex websites for larger clients for the 3 years 
• Set department standards and processes

• Helped the company save time and money by implementing a WordPress site manager which made the work of managing 150+ clients easier for multiple departments 
• Created a custom page builder using Advanced Custom Fields to ensure quality of sites what similar regardless of which developer built the site 
• Collaborated with other department heads to streamline integration between design, account management, and web.
